summary: Large GX matching pass that replaced broad asm/inc-only scaffolding with C/header declarations for Dolphin GX and related OS types. The PR deleted the old include/dolphin/gx/GX.inc and several .inc stubs, added include/dolphin/gx/gxprivate.h plus public GX headers, introduced src/dolphin/gx/*.c placeholder/declaration units, moved PPCArch/OSThread-style headers toward include/dolphin, and retouched baselib callers to use the new GX/OS types. Diff evidence is mostly structural and symbol/prototype cleanup rather than reviewer discussion.
what_changed: Deleted include/dolphin/gx/GX.inc and other inc fragments such as include/dolphin/gx/GXInit.inc, GXFifo.inc, OSThread.inc, and OSContext.inc while adding typed C headers under include/dolphin.

Added include/dolphin/gx/gxprivate.h with a temporary private GX dependency hub, including __GXFifoObj, GXContext, GXContexts, WGPIPE/WGPIPE_OP MMIO declarations, __GXGPFifo, __GXContexts, FIFO globals, and internal GX prototypes.
Changed many GX asm references from anonymous lbl_804D5BA8 to the named global __GXContexts, making the GX context pointer searchable and reusable across files.
Marked some previously global helper labels as local labels in asm, for example func_8033D1B8 became local_8033D1B8 and func_8033D3E4 became local_8033D3E4, with callers updated accordingly.
Added decomp.me scratch comments above numerous GX asm functions, recording scratch IDs and progress percentages such as mHuNc, CwsOr, vLLix, Vrpb1, sAjjF, and others.

Expanded gxtypes.h formatting and typing, including changing GXColor from a plain struct to a union containing both byte channels and a u32 view, and moving GXLightObj out of the old dummy array pattern into a more specific light-object layout in GXLight.h.

Added public GX headers for light, attr, fifo, init, misc, and umbrella GX declarations, with prototypes for GX light/color/channel APIs such as GXInitLightColor, GXLoadLightObjImm, GXSetChanAmbColor, GXSetChanMatColor, GXSetNumChans, and GXSetChanCtrl.
Added src/dolphin/gx/GX.c and small GX module C files that collect extern declarations/prototypes for GX functions, including many address-commented symbols still named func_8033xxxx.

Added include/dolphin/os/OSThread.h with OSThreadState, OSThreadFlag, OSThreadQueue, OSMutexQueue, OSThread, OS_CURRENT_THREAD/OS_THREAD_QUEUE, and thread API prototypes; added related OSInterrupt, OSMutex, OSContext, and PPCArch header material.
Updated sysdolphin/baselib/initialize.c to include <dolphin/gx/GXLight.h>, use pointer-style formatting consistently, and strengthen HSD_AllocateFifo from void* to GXFifoObj*.
Removed a large block of ad hoc declarations from include/functions.h and include/variables.h as declarations moved into subsystem headers.
smart_moves: Naming lbl_804D5BA8 as __GXContexts in asm creates a high-value anchor for future GX decompilation and clarifies many repeated SDA loads without needing full C matches immediately.
Introducing gxprivate.h acknowledged unresolved GX interdependencies explicitly with a TODO, while still unblocking typed declarations and matching work.
Separating public GX headers like GXLight.h/GXFifo.h/GXAttr.h from private context/FIFO internals provides a path away from monolithic GX.inc declarations.
Using union GXColor with both channel and u32 access is a practical matching aid for code that treats colors either as four bytes or a packed word.
Replacing dummy GXLightObj storage with offset-commented fields makes light initialization/load functions more tractable and reduces opaque pointer arithmetic.
Keeping decomp.me scratch URLs and progress percentages in asm preserves provenance for later matchers and helps identify which functions were already investigated.
Retaining local_ labels for non-exported asm helpers avoids over-promoting internal code to public symbols while preserving branch targets.
decomp_lessons: For large library subsystems, first stabilize global context names and core structs; even imperfect layouts with offset comments are more useful than anonymous labels and untyped void pointers.
Move declarations out of catch-all include/functions.h and include/variables.h into subsystem headers as soon as enough types exist, because that improves include hygiene and searchability.

When matching Dolphin GX, expect many functions to share the same __GXContexts state, dirty flag field, and WGPIPE register writes; naming these patterns early pays off across GXAttr, GXFifo, GXInit, GXMisc, and GX.c.
Private headers can be acceptable as temporary scaffolding if they document that interdependencies are not final and isolate internals from public API headers.
For hardware/MMIO code, typed volatile declarations with fixed addresses under M2C guards can support decompilation while preserving build-context flexibility.
Changing a type such as GXColor can ripple through baselib, lobj/mobj/tobj/tev, lbrefract, and stage/game code; expect cross-system cleanup in GX matching passes.
Offset comments in structs like GXContext, OSThread, and GXLightObj are valuable evidence for future field naming, even when many fields remain xNN or padding.
naming_conventions: Anonymous SDA label lbl_804D5BA8 was renamed to __GXContexts for the GX global context container.
Internal helper labels that are not public APIs were renamed with a local_8033XXXX pattern, shown by local_8033D1B8 and local_8033D3E4.
GX private/internal symbols follow double-underscore naming where known, for example __GXSetDirtyState, __GXSetVCD, __GXSetVAT, __GXFifoInit, __GXFifoObj, and __GXGPFifo.
Public Dolphin GX types keep official-looking GX-prefixed names such as GXRenderModeObj, GXFifoObj, GXColor, GXLightObj, GXChannelID, GXColorSrc, GXDiffuseFn, GXAttnFn, and GXLightID.
Unknown or partially known struct fields in new headers use x-offset names and padding arrays such as x4F0, x570, xBC_pad, and x1C_data.
OS header names follow Dolphin SDK-style names such as OSThread, OSThreadQueue, OSMutexQueue, OSThreadState, OS_THREAD_STATE_RUNNING, and OS_THREAD_STACK_MAGIC.
Many unmatched functions remain address-derived func_8033XXXX names with address comments rather than speculative semantic names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted decomp.me scratch links directly above asm functions to document matching attempts and progress percentages.
Replaced repeated asm loads of lbl_804D5BA8@sda21(r13) with __GXContexts@sda21(r13), improving symbol identity without changing instruction bytes.
Changed branch targets to local labels where helpers were not exported, reducing symbol namespace pollution while keeping assembly control flow intact.
Added module-level C files containing extern declarations and prototypes for asm-resident GX functions, a bridge tactic before full C replacement.
Used fixed-address volatile WGPIPE and WGPIPE_OP declarations to model GX FIFO/MMIO accesses in C-facing headers.
Removed legacy .inc data/prototype fragments after equivalent typed headers existed, reducing duplicate declaration sources.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove or rework include/dolphin/gx/gxprivate.h after GX interdependencies are resolved.
Search later GX passes for __GXContexts field renames from x-offset names to semantic GXContext fields.
Look for PRs that fully match GXInitLightColor, GXLoadLightObjImm, GXSetChanAmbColor, GXSetChanMatColor, and GXSetChanCtrl.
Trace whether GXColor as a union caused follow-up callsite changes in baselib lobj, mobj, tobj, tev, lbrefract, or ground.
Search for cleanup of duplicate or mistaken extern declarations in src/dolphin/gx/GX.c, especially repeated func_80340790.
